perPage.summary: This is the app's central work surface: a split-panel parts workspace (left = filterable parts table via parts/list.html, right = detail tabs for the selected part). The table-level CSS primitives (.compact-table, .panel-header, .ws-tab, .badge family) are good and the offers/sourcing tabs partly adopt the named cell utilities (.table-cell, .compact-cell). But the filter/toolbar and pagination in list.html are hand-rolled with brand-500 active states, gray-* hairlines, and a wall of text-[10px]/text-[9px] that sits below the new 11px readability floor — this is the single largest pocket of foundation-debt on the page. Against the philosophy it has the right bones (dense, keyboard-ish, institutional memory in the sourcing tab's score/tier/phone) but fails the Three-Second Rule on "Where am I?" (no title/breadcrumb) and "What needs my attention?" (the Bid Due column has zero urgency treatment), and the offers tab — the decision surface — shows no vendor-trust signal at all.

findings.issue: The entire left-panel toolbar (Add Req button, search input, status filter pills, bulk-action bar, pagination, undo countdown) is sized text-[10px], and two table cells (owner avatar initial, spec /6 suffix) are text-[9px]. The PR0 foundation sets a hard readability floor of 'no body copy < 11px'. ~14 distinct controls/labels here violate it — the densest, most-used controls on the app's home page are the least legible.
findings.fix: Bump these to text-[11px] (the .chip/.badge-mini token size) or text-xs. The search input should adopt .input/.input-sm (which is text-xs + the accent focus ring) rather than a bespoke text-[10px] input. Keep the avatar circle but raise the initial to text-[10px] minimum, or use text-[11px] leading-none.

findings.issue: Active/primary states use brand-* instead of the new accent. Status filter pills go 'bg-brand-500 text-white' when active (line 52), the Add Req CTA is bg-brand-500 (line 24), the bulk-action bar is bg-brand-50/border-brand-200/text-brand-700 (lines 67-68), and select-checkboxes are text-brand-500. Per PR0, any ACTIVE state, PRIMARY cta, or focus ring must resolve to accent-*; brand-* is only correct for neutral chrome.
findings.fix: Active filter pill → accent-500/accent-600; Add Req → .btn-primary (or accent-500). Bulk bar → accent-50/accent-200/accent-700. Checkbox accent color → text-accent-500. Leave neutral card/border brand usage alone.

findings.issue: Search input uses a bespoke 'focus:ring-1 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500' focus spec instead of the foundation's single .input focus contract (ring-2, --accent-ring, accent border). Focus rings are explicitly called out in PR0 as needing to move to accent.
findings.fix: Replace the input's border/focus classes with .input .input-sm (resolves to the accent focus ring) and drop the inline ring-brand-500 classes. Same for the inner search SVG positioning which can stay.

findings.issue: The active detail-tab indicator (color #3A4252 + 2px slate underline) and the tab-bar/pagination separators 'border-brand-200' (workspace.html:100, list.html:212) are slate/brand, not accent. The active tab is the canonical 'active state' the foundation says should read as the one accent.
findings.fix: Set .ws-tab-active color + border-bottom-color to var(--accent). Convert the 'border-b-2 border-brand-200' tab bar and 'border-t-2 border-brand-200' pagination strips to .border-line-base (or accent if you want them to echo the active tab).

findings.issue: The Add Req button, the six status filter pills, and the Prev/Next pagination buttons re-implement the .btn / .chip / .filter-pill primitives inline (px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold rounded + hand-rolled active/hover color logic) instead of using the shared component vocabulary. This is the 'bespoke markup re-implementing a primitive' smell and is why this toolbar drifts from sibling pages.
findings.fix: Render the status filters with the shared filter-pill/.chip pattern (toggle accent active), the Add Req button as .btn-primary .btn-sm, and Prev/Next as .btn-secondary .btn-sm. Drives consistency and fixes the accent + readability debt in one pass.

findings.issue: Hairlines across the cluster use border-gray-300 / border-gray-200 / divide-gray-200 / divide-gray-100 instead of the line tokens. PR0 is draining the legacy border-gray-* override page-by-page; this page still has 32 such occurrences. The part header strip (header.html:8) and both detail-tab table wrappers are gray-bordered.
findings.fix: Convert hairlines to .border-line-base / .border-line-subtle (and for divide, swap to the compact-table/data-table classes which already carry the #BFC4CE border). The two detail-tab tables could drop their bespoke 'min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200' wrapper and adopt .compact-table outright.

findings.issue: The Score and Tier cells inline 'px-3 py-2' while every sibling cell in the same table uses .compact-cell (5px 10px). The two inlined cells render looser than the rest of the row, breaking the density lock the rest of the table already honors.
findings.fix: Replace 'px-3 py-2 whitespace-nowrap' with 'compact-cell whitespace-nowrap' on both <td>s so the row is uniform.

findings.issue: Three-Second-Rule 'What needs my attention?' failure: the Bid Due (need_by_date) column renders raw '{{ req.need_by_date or '—' }}' with no urgency treatment — overdue, due-today, and due-next-month all look identical. For a broker racing bid deadlines this is the single most decision-relevant column and it carries zero visual signal.
findings.fix: Compute days-to-due and apply a semantic treatment: overdue → text-rose-600 font-semibold (or .badge-warning), due ≤2 days → amber, else neutral. Mirror the existing spec-count badge pattern so it stays compact. This is the highest buyer-payoff change on the page.

findings.location: app/templates/htmx/partials/parts/workspace.html:8-19 (pipeline strip) and absence of any page title
findings.issue: Three-Second-Rule 'Where am I?': the page has no title or breadcrumb. Identity rests entirely on the bottom-nav active label. The only header element is the 26px pipeline strip, and its key figures (open value, weighted forecast) are rendered as plain text-gray-700, not emphasized — the foundation provides .figure-accent precisely for hero numerics like these.
findings.fix: Add a lightweight title/eyebrow ('Sales Hub' or 'Requisitions') to the pipeline strip or panel-header so the page self-identifies on direct load. Apply .figure-accent to the open-value and weighted-forecast numbers so the institutional-memory figures read as the page's key metrics.

findings.issue: Trust-must-be-visible failure on the decision surface: the offers table shows vendor_name as plain text with no trust/safety signal ('delivered before' vs 'never heard of them'). The sourcing tab already surfaces score/tier/phone per vendor, but the offers tab — where the buyer actually picks who to buy from — shows none of it. A stale tag exists (line 43-44) but vendor reliability does not.
findings.fix: Add a vendor-trust indicator to the Vendor cell (e.g. a small .badge-success 'Known' / neutral 'New' chip, or the same tier the sourcing tab computes), reusing the existing scoring/tier data rather than a new primitive. Keeps 'the phone is the weapon' and trust-at-a-glance consistent across both tabs.

findings.issue: Offer status uses raw 'bg-green-100 text-green-700' / 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-700' / 'bg-gray-100' spans plus a one-off amber Stale tag, instead of the locked .badge family (.badge-success/.badge-info/.badge). The adjacent sourcing tab uses yet another color map (bg-emerald-50/bg-blue-50). Two tabs of the same workspace render status badges three different ways.
findings.fix: Route both tabs' status badges through the .badge / .badge-success / .badge-info / .badge-warning helpers (or the shared status_badge macro already imported elsewhere) so offer status, sighting status, and the table status pill share one vocabulary.

findings.issue: Drag-handle hover/active uses brand-400/brand-500 (workspace.html:76-77) and the pipeline strip + parts-list wrapper use border-gray-200 (workspace.html:12, list.html implicit). The resize handle's active state is an interactive accent moment that should read as the accent.
findings.fix: Drag handle hover → bg-accent-400, dragging → bg-accent-500. Pipeline strip border-gray-200 → .border-line-base. Low impact but completes the accent/line sweep for this template.

findings.issue: The 'No parts found' empty state has no primary action — it tells the buyer to 'Adjust filters or add requirements' but offers no button, even though the Add Req CTA exists 180px above in the toolbar. Three-Second-Rule 'What next?' is unanswered when the list is empty (the most likely first-run / fresh-DB state per project notes).
findings.fix: Add an 'Add Requisition' .btn-primary .btn-sm inside the empty state that dispatches the same open-modal event as the toolbar button, so a zero-state buyer has a one-click path forward.

topOpportunities: Refactor the left-panel toolbar + pagination in list.html in one pass: convert the hand-rolled text-[10px] brand-500 filter pills / Add Req / Prev-Next to the shared .btn-* / .chip / filter-pill primitives with accent active states and the .input search field. This single edit clears the page's biggest readability-floor violation, the largest accent foundation-debt pocket, AND the worst consistency smell simultaneously.

Add urgency treatment to the Bid Due (need_by_date) column (overdue=rose, due-soon=amber). Highest buyer-workflow payoff per unit effort — it makes the home table answer 'what needs my attention?' at a glance for deadline-driven bid work, which it currently cannot.

Surface a vendor-trust signal on the Offers tab (reuse the score/tier the Sightings tab already computes). The offers tab is the actual buy-decision surface and today shows no 'delivered before vs never heard of them' cue, violating the trust-at-a-glance principle that is core to a broker sourcing tool.

Run the line-token + accent sweep across the cluster (32 border-gray-* → .border-line-*, the slate .ws-tab-active and border-brand-200 strips → accent) so the workspace stops drifting from the rest of the post-PR0 app; mostly mechanical S/M effort that locks the page onto the new baseline.

perPage.summary: Sightings is a mature, dense split-panel command view that already adopts much of the foundation: the vendor table in detail.html uses .compact-cell--head, primary CTAs in detail.html / offers_panel.html use .btn-primary/.btn-sm, status pills route through the shared status_badge macro, and the empty state reuses the shared partial. The gap is almost entirely in form controls and the RFQ modal: nearly every input/select/textarea across table.html, vendor_modal.html, activity_section.html, offer_form_modal.html and qual_request_modal.html still hand-rolls focus:ring-brand-* / focus:border-brand-* instead of the .input class, so focus rings have NOT moved to the accent — the most pervasive piece of foundation-debt on the page. The modal's terminal "Send to N Vendors" CTA is bespoke bg-brand-500/600 rather than .btn-primary, and the detail-panel tab indicator + Build-RFQ link use brand-500/600 for active/primary states. Hierarchy is strong (priority dashboard strip answers "what needs attention" in <3s, phone is one tap on every vendor row), so the work here is conformance polish, not restructuring.

findings.issue: The two primary filter inputs (Search MPN/customer, Manufacturer) hand-roll their focus state as focus:border-brand-400 focus:ring-1/ring-2 focus:ring-brand-200 instead of using the .input class. Post-PR0 the canonical focus ring resolves to the accent (--accent-ring) via .input/.input-focus; these are the most-used controls on the page and still glow brand, not azure — visible foundation-debt every time the buyer searches.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke class string on both inputs with `class="input"` (drop border/focus utilities). .input already supplies w-full px-3 py-1.5 text-sm, the gray-300 border, and the accent focus ring. Keep the flex-1 min-w / w-[140px] width utility by appending it to the class list (e.g. class="input flex-1 min-w-[150px]").

findings.issue: The modal's terminal primary action — 'Send to N Vendors' (compose step) and 'Confirm & Send' (preview step) — is bespoke `bg-brand-500 hover:bg-brand-600` (compose) and `bg-green-600 hover:bg-green-700` (preview). Neither resolves to the accent; the single most important CTA in the RFQ flow is off-token, and the two send buttons don't even match each other.
findings.fix: Swap both to `class="btn-primary"` (add the disabled:* utilities back via Alpine :disabled which already exists). This moves the send CTA onto the azure accent and unifies the two-step flow. The Cancel/Back buttons should become .btn-secondary for the same reason (lines 278, 315).

findings.issue: The activity log's type-select, title input and note textarea inline `focus:border-brand-400 focus:ring-brand-400` with rounded-md/border-gray-300 rather than .input. Same brand-not-accent focus-ring debt as the filter bar, and the rounded-md diverges from the .input rounded-lg standard.
findings.fix: Replace each control's class with `input input-sm` (text-xs py-1.5 px-2 maps cleanly to input-sm); for the textarea add `resize-none`. This drops the brand focus ring, the ad-hoc rounded-md, and the hardcoded border-gray-300 in one move.

findings.issue: The detail-panel tab indicator uses border-brand-500 text-brand-500 for the ACTIVE tab. Per PR0 an active-state indicator is exactly what should move to the accent; the active Vendors/Offers/Activity tab currently reads brand, not azure.
findings.fix: Change the active branch from 'border-brand-500 text-brand-500' to use the accent — e.g. style the active state with the accent var (class 'text-accent-600 border-accent-600' if the accent-* Tailwind family is safelisted, matching how active bottom-nav resolves). Inactive 'text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-900' stays.

findings.issue: Build RFQ is a primary action correctly using .btn-primary, but the Customer link (line 39) and the inline status <select> hover (line 60 hover:border-brand-400) use brand-600/brand-400 for an interactive/primary accent. The Build-RFQ pill in _vendor_row.html:157 likewise uses border-brand-200 text-brand-600 hover:bg-brand-50 — a primary 'phone is the weapon' action styled in brand, not accent.
findings.fix: Move the interactive-accent usages to accent-*: customer link text-accent-600, the status select hover:border-accent-400, and the per-vendor Build RFQ pill to text-accent-600 border-accent-200 hover:bg-accent-50. Leaves neutral chrome (gray borders/cards) on brand-*/gray-* as intended.

findings.issue: The offer-entry, qual-request and add-vendor-contact form fields inline `focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500` (and rounded, not rounded-lg) rather than .input. These are the offer-capture and add-vendor surfaces — low traffic but they perpetuate the brand-focus-ring debt and a divergent corner radius.
findings.fix: Replace each field's class with `input` (or `input input-sm` for the compact add-vendor fields). Normalizes focus ring to accent and radius to rounded-lg across all sightings forms.

findings.issue: The requirements <table class="compact-table"> inlines px-3 py-2 / px-2 py-2 on every <th> and <td>. The unlayered .compact-table th/td CSS lock (styles.css:415,431 — padding 7px/5px 10px) overrides these equal-specificity utilities, so the inline paddings are dead weight that masks the lock and diverges from the sibling vendor table in detail.html which correctly uses .compact-cell--head. Cosmetically fine today, but it's exactly the inlined-cell-padding pattern the foundation wants drained, and it invites drift if the lock ever changes specificity.
findings.fix: Strip the px-*/py-* utilities from the thead <th> and tbody <td> elements (keep text-left/right/center, widths, and hidden-breakpoint classes). Let .compact-table own row padding, matching detail.html. The checkbox cells can use .compact-cell if explicit padding is wanted.

findings.issue: The priority dashboard strip (Urgent/Stale/Pending) and the status pills (lines 51-58) hand-roll pill markup with one-off bg-rose-100/amber-100/blue-100 + px-3 py-1 rounded-full, while the rest of the app has .badge/.chip/.badge-mini primitives and a status_badge macro. The counters and pills re-implement a primitive with bespoke color maps, so they don't track the shared badge family or the unified 11px uppercase header type.
findings.fix: Keep the hx-get button behavior but render the visual pill via .chip/.badge (or .badge-warning/.badge-info helpers) so color, padding, radius and border come from the locked family. At minimum align the status pills (line 54) to the same px/text scale as .chip rather than text-[10px]/[9px] one-offs.

findings.issue: Vendor-status pills, OOO/overlap/sub-MPN badges and offer-status pills are all rendered as raw `inline-flex px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] rounded-full {color}` literals with per-file color maps, instead of the .badge/.chip primitives. The same 'status pill' concept is re-implemented in at least three files with slightly different padding/text sizes, so the badge vocabulary drifts row-to-row.
findings.fix: Introduce a small shared macro (or reuse status_badge with a status_map) for the vendor-status and offer-status pills so the color maps live once; render the metadata badges (OOO, overlap, via-MPN) through .chip/.badge-mini. Non-blocking but removes triplicated color dictionaries.

findings.issue: Key figures in the detail header — Qty and Target price — are rendered at text-xs font-mono text-gray-700, visually identical to their labels. The buyer's two most decision-relevant numbers (how many, target $) get no emphasis, missing the foundation's .figure-accent hero-numeric treatment and weakening the 3-second 'is this worth my time' read.
findings.fix: Bump the Qty and Target values to a larger/heavier weight (e.g. text-sm font-semibold) and consider .figure-accent on the target price so the hero numbers stand out from the gray labels. Coverage % already gets a bar; the header numbers should match that emphasis.

findings.issue: Requirement rows are click-to-select via @click="selectReq(id)" on a <tr> with cursor-pointer but no role/tabindex/keydown handler, so the primary navigation of the page is mouse-only — a keyboard user cannot select a requirement to load the detail panel. The foundation calls out keyboard nav on rows as a lens.
findings.fix: Add `tabindex="0" role="button"` and `@keydown.enter="selectReq({{ r.id }})"` (and space) to the row, mirroring the accent focus-ring so the focused row is visible. Ensure the row's focus outline uses the accent ring rather than being suppressed.

findings.issue: The mobile card selected-state uses ring-2 ring-brand-300; selection/active emphasis is now an accent concern. Minor, but it's a brand-* active state on a selection indicator.
findings.fix: Change to ring-2 with the accent ring color (ring-accent-300 if safelisted, or the row-selected token) to match the desktop row-selected treatment and the accent foundation.

topOpportunities: Adopt .input everywhere (table.html filter bar, vendor_modal, activity_section, offer/qual modals): one find-replace sweep moves every form-control focus ring from brand to the azure accent and unifies radius — the single highest-leverage foundation conformance fix on the page, and the filter inputs are touched on nearly every visit.

Put the RFQ-send flow on the accent: switch vendor_modal.html's 'Send to N Vendors' / 'Confirm & Send' to .btn-primary and Cancel/Back to .btn-secondary. This is the terminal CTA of the core 'phone is the weapon' RFQ workflow and is currently both off-token and internally inconsistent (brand-500 vs green-600).

Make the requirements rows keyboard-navigable (tabindex/role/@keydown.enter on the <tr> in table.html): the page's primary interaction — selecting a requirement to load the detail panel — is mouse-only today, blocking keyboard-driven buyers the Bloomberg-terminal doctrine targets.

Move active/interactive-accent states off brand-* in detail.html (active tab indicator, customer link, inline status select) and the per-vendor Build RFQ pill, so 'what's active / what to click next' reads in the one azure accent rather than the legacy brand blue.

perPage.summary: Materials is a dense, well-structured faceted-search workspace that already honors much of the foundation: results use .compact-table compact-table--dense (correct cell locks), .badge for status/lifecycle/condition pills, the mobile "Show Results" button uses .btn-primary, empty/loading/error states exist, and rows are keyboard-operable. The dominant gap is accent migration: the entire surface predates PR0 and still resolves every ACTIVE state, focus ring, primary CTA, and key figure to brand-500/600/700/800 instead of accent-* — so this page reads in the old brand hue while the rest of the app moves to Trio azure. Secondary debt is bespoke markup re-implementing primitives (the desktop "Add part" button, sidebar inputs, and active-filter pills hand-roll .btn-primary/.input/.chip) and inlined px-4/py-2 paddings on the search-bar and detail header that run looser than the new density locks.

findings.issue: Every ACTIVE/selected/focus state in the sidebar resolves to brand-* not accent-*: 'Clear all'/'Copy link' (brand-600), recent-commodity active chip (bg-brand-100 text-brand-800 border-brand-300), category-search focus ring (focus:ring-brand-500), 'All Materials' active state (bg-brand-100 text-brand-800 border-brand-500), confidence/sourcing checkboxes (text-brand-600 focus:ring-brand-500), Part-type/Searched-within toggles active (bg-brand-50 text-brand-700 border-brand-300), Min-searches input focus ring. Per PR0 these active/focus states must move to accent-*.
findings.fix: Swap brand-→accent- for selected/active/focus only: text-brand-600→text-accent-600, bg-brand-100 text-brand-800 border-brand-300/500→bg-accent-100 text-accent-800 border-accent-300/500, focus:ring-brand-500→focus:ring-accent-500 (or apply .input/.input-focus which already carry the accent ring), text-brand-600 focus:ring-brand-500 on checkboxes→text-accent-600 focus:ring-accent-500, bg-brand-50 text-brand-700 border-brand-300→bg-accent-50 text-accent-700 border-accent-300. Leave neutral chrome/borders on brand-*.

findings.issue: The primary 'Add part' CTA hand-rolls bg-brand-500 ... hover:bg-brand-600 instead of the .btn-primary primitive, so it stays brand-blue while the foundation's primary accent is Trio azure; the mobile filter-count badge is bg-brand-500; and the search input uses focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500. The sibling mobile 'Show Results' button (line 263) already uses .btn-primary, making the desktop CTA inconsistent within the same template.
findings.fix: Replace the Add-part button's bg-brand-500/hover:bg-brand-600 with .btn-primary (keep the icon spans + flex-shrink-0). Change the mobile filter badge bg-brand-500→bg-accent-500. Apply class .input (or swap focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500→accent) to the search input so its focus ring resolves to --accent like the rest of the app.

findings.issue: Filter macros (rendered on every page load) bake brand-* into active/focus states: facet search-box focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500, checkbox text-brand-600 focus:ring-brand-500, 'Show all' toggle text-brand-600 hover:text-brand-700, numeric/boolean selected chips bg-brand-50 text-brand-700 border-brand-300, range-input focus:ring-brand-500, and the disclosure_header active-count badge bg-brand-100 text-brand-700. These are all active/focus surfaces that PR0 routes to accent-*.
findings.fix: Migrate the active/focus tokens to accent-*: focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500→accent (or use .input on the search/range inputs), checkbox text-brand-600 focus:ring-brand-500→text-accent-600 focus:ring-accent-500, selected-chip bg-brand-50 text-brand-700 border-brand-300→bg-accent-50 text-accent-700 border-accent-300, 'Show all' text-brand-600→text-accent-600, header count badge bg-brand-100 text-brand-700→bg-accent-100 text-accent-700.

findings.issue: The commodity-tree active row (the primary navigation selection for the whole page) uses bg-brand-100 text-brand-800 font-semibold border-l-[3px] border-brand-500 — an active state still in the old brand hue. This is the single most-clicked selector on the page, so it visibly reads off-accent.
findings.fix: Change the active branch to bg-accent-100 text-accent-800 ... border-accent-500; leave the inactive branch's neutral text-gray-600 untouched.

findings.issue: Detail page key figure + CTAs + active tab are still brand-*: the Searches count (the page's hero numeric) is font-bold text-brand-600 rather than the opt-in .figure-accent; Enrich/Insights buttons and the Save submit hand-roll text-brand-600 bg-brand-50 / bg-brand-500 hover:bg-brand-600 instead of primitives; the active tab indicator is border-brand-500 text-brand-600; edit-form inputs use focus:ring-brand-500.
findings.fix: Wrap the Searches value in .figure-accent. Convert the Save submit to .btn-primary. Move Enrich/Insights to accent-tinted ghost styling (text-accent-600 bg-accent-50 border-accent-200) or .btn-secondary. Active tab→border-accent-500 text-accent-600. Edit-input focus rings→accent or class .input.

findings.issue: The active-filter chips re-implement a primitive by hand: each is inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 py-0.5 ...-50 text-...-700 rounded-full text-xs across ~12 near-duplicate blocks (blue-50, amber-50, gray-100, brand-50). The foundation provides .badge (rounded-full status pill) and .chip (rectangular metadata tag); hand-rolled markup means each chip can drift in padding/size and none picks up the locked badge sizing.
findings.fix: Collapse the removable filter chips onto the .chip (or .badge) primitive plus a semantic color override, e.g. …, and route the commodity/AI chips' accent color through accent-* (bg-accent-50 text-accent-700). One macro for 'removable chip(label, onRemove, color)' would dedupe all ~12 blocks.

findings.issue: The results-area search bar header inlines p-4 and the buttons inline px-3 py-2, which is looser than the new locks (<main> is p-3, .btn is px-3.5 py-1.5). The detail hero (detail.html:15 p-4 mb-6) and tab buttons (px-4 py-2.5) likewise run loose. Inlined paddings here visibly diverge from the tightened standard elsewhere.
findings.fix: Bring the search-bar container to p-3 (matching <main>), let the Add-part/filter buttons inherit .btn padding via the primitives, and tighten detail.html section paddings/margins (p-4→p-3, mb-6→mb-4) toward the density locks. Use the named cell/btn utilities rather than re-inlining px/py.

findings.issue: Hairlines/dividers use border-gray-100/200 and the legacy override block rather than the line tokens, and the results table + cards use border-brand-200 for their hairline frame. PR0 introduces .border-line-base / .border-line-subtle (--line #BFC4CE / --line-subtle #D5D8DF) to drain the border-gray-* !important block page-by-page.
findings.fix: Convert structural hairlines (sidebar border-r, section border-t dividers, search-bar border-b, table/card frame, tab border-b, pagination button borders) from border-gray-100/200 and border-brand-200 to .border-line-subtle (light dividers) or .border-line-base (table/card frame).

findings.issue: Three-Second-Rule 'Where am I?' is weak: the workspace root renders straight into the filter/results columns with no page title or breadcrumb (the only label is the bottom-nav). The result count ('N results in <commodity>') is the closest thing to an at-a-glance answer but sits small (text-sm) inside the results scroll area, and there is no count/urgency cue for the high-value 'Needs review' (validation-conflict) queue at the page level — it's only reachable as a buried sidebar toggle.
findings.fix: Add a compact page header (title 'Materials' + active-commodity breadcrumb) above the two columns, and surface a 'Needs review (N)' attention chip near the search bar that toggles needsReview — turning the institutional-memory review queue into a glanceable, one-click action instead of a hidden filter.

findings.issue: Result rows are role=button tabindex=0 with focus:ring-brand-500 (focus ring still in the old brand hue, not the accent the foundation standardizes on). Functionally accessible, but the focus indicator won't match the app-wide accent focus treatment.
findings.fix: Change the row focus ring focus:ring-brand-500→focus:ring-accent-500 (focus:ring-inset retained) so keyboard focus matches the foundation's accent focus spec.

findings.issue: Description/Manufacturer/Last-Seen cells add inline truncate max-w-[200px]/[160px] and a category sub-line at text-[11px]; the MPN cell uses font-data font-semibold text-brand-600 (the MPN, a key identifier, is colored in the old brand hue). The 11px sub-line is at the readability floor and the brand-tinted MPN is an active-ish emphasis that PR0 would route to accent.
findings.fix: Keep truncation but move the prominent MPN's text-brand-600 to text-accent-600 (or leave neutral gray-900 and reserve accent for genuinely interactive text) for consistency with the foundation's single-accent rule; confirm the category sub-line stays >=11px (it is exactly at the floor).

findings.issue: Pagination buttons (Prev/Next/page-range) are bespoke px-3 py-1.5 ... border border-brand-200 rounded-lg hover:bg-brand-50 rather than the .btn-secondary primitive, and use brand-* hover. Minor, but it's another primitive re-implementation that will drift from the locked button sizing/hover.
findings.fix: Replace Prev/Next with .btn-secondary .btn-sm; if a brand hover tint is desired keep it neutral (hover:bg-gray-50) per the secondary spec, removing the bespoke brand-200/brand-50 styling.

topOpportunities: Run a single accent-migration pass across workspace.html, _macros.html, tree.html and detail.html: every ACTIVE/selected/focus/primary brand-500/600/700/800 → accent-* (and the Searches hero numeric → .figure-accent). This is the highest-leverage move — it's the bulk of the page's foundation-debt, mechanically safe (accent-* is already safelisted), and instantly aligns the most-used sourcing surface with the new app-wide azure.

Adopt the primitives for the buttons and chips: convert the desktop 'Add part' CTA and detail Save to .btn-primary, Prev/Next to .btn-secondary, and collapse the ~12 hand-rolled active-filter chips onto a single .chip/.badge macro. Removes drift and guarantees the locked sizing/hover everywhere.

Add a lightweight page header (title 'Materials' + active-commodity breadcrumb) and surface a glanceable 'Needs review (N)' attention chip by the search bar — closing the Three-Second-Rule 'where am I / what needs attention' gaps and turning the validation-conflict review queue into a one-click action instead of a buried sidebar toggle.

Drain the line-token debt on this page: convert structural hairlines (sidebar/section dividers, search-bar/table/card/tab frames) from border-gray-*/border-brand-200 to .border-line-subtle/.border-line-base, and tighten the inlined p-4/px-4 py-2.5 paddings toward the new density locks (p-3, primitive button padding).

perPage.summary: The Part Dossier ("The Bench") is one of the best-architected surfaces in the app: instant DB-read hero, lazy-loaded sections with explicit hx-target guards, a reused lead-detail drawer, a two-tier sticky search/rail, and genuinely strong empty/loading/degraded states (the SSE empty-state pivots to "what we know," the degraded-source banner is exemplary). Its hierarchy mostly satisfies the Three-Second Rule — title (mono MPN H1), attention cues (knowledge tiles, AUTH/confidence badges, "New to us"), and a prominent Send-RFQ primary are all present. The problem is purely foundation timing: this page was authored just before PR0 and never migrated, so every primary CTA still hardcodes brand-600/700 instead of .btn-primary/--accent, the tier-1 search input uses focus:ring-brand-500 instead of .input/accent-ring, hairlines are border-gray-*, and hero key-figures don't use .figure-accent. The landing form.html already adopted .btn-primary, which proves the rest is migratable debt, not a constraint.

findings.issue: The page's single most important CTA — hero 'Send RFQ' — hardcodes bg-brand-600 hover:bg-brand-700 instead of the accent. Under PR0 the primary CTA must resolve to --accent (Trio azure) via .btn-primary, so this key action now renders in the wrong (legacy brand) color vs every other page's primary button.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke class list with `btn-primary` (keep the inline-flex/gap/icon and shadow-sm if desired: `btn-primary shadow-sm`). Drops 6 utility classes and adopts the accent + accent focus ring automatically.

findings.issue: Three more primary CTAs on bg-brand-600 hover:bg-brand-700: the tier-1 sticky search 'Search' button (line 26), the condensed-rail 'Send RFQ' (line 42), and the mobile action-sheet 'Send RFQ' (line 171). All are primary actions that PR0 says must be accent, but they render in legacy brand blue.
findings.fix: Convert each to `.btn-primary` with size modifiers: line 26 → `btn-primary btn-sm`; line 42 → `btn-primary btn-sm` (keep the inline-flex icon); line 171 → `btn-primary flex-1` (full-width). This both fixes the accent and collapses the duplicated padding/color soup to one primitive.

findings.issue: The drawer's 'Add to Shortlist' primary action is bg-brand-600 hover:bg-brand-700 — same legacy-brand-instead-of-accent debt. The drawer is the per-vendor decision surface, so its primary should be accent.
findings.fix: Replace the class with `btn-primary flex-1` (the wrapper already provides the flex row).

findings.issue: The landing search input uses `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500` plus a hand-rolled border/padding stack instead of the .input primitive. PR0 made .input the single focus spec (ring-2 + accent ring + accent border). This is the page's primary entry control and its focus ring is legacy brand, not accent.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke classes with `input font-mono tracking-tight` (keep flex-1 via a wrapper or `class="input ..."`). The .input class already supplies px-3 py-1.5, border, rounded-lg, accent focus ring, and accent focus border.

findings.issue: The tier-1 sticky-bar part-number input is a bare bg-transparent input with `placeholder:text-gray-400 outline-none` and NO focus ring at all — keyboard users get zero visible focus affordance on the dossier's most-used control (deep-link a new PN). This is both an a11y gap and an accent-token miss.
findings.fix: Add the accent focus mixin: append `input-focus rounded-md` (input-focus = focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 with accent ring + accent border on :focus) so the transparent bar still shows an accent ring on keyboard focus.

findings.issue: The two hero key-figures buyers scan first — the Market price (line 79, font-mono text-2xl) and the knowledge count numerals (lines 108-111, font-mono text-lg) — are plain text-brand-900, not the new .figure-accent. PR0 introduced .figure-accent specifically for hero numerics so the decision-driving number reads as the accent and gets tabular-nums alignment.
findings.fix: Add `.figure-accent` to the market-price <span> (line 79) and to the four count numerals (lines 108-111) where the value is non-zero (keep text-gray-300 for the zero/empty case). Gives tabular-nums + accent emphasis on the figures buyers triage by.

findings.issue: The price sparkline hardcodes inline hex colors (stroke="#838B9B", fill="#4B5463") in the SVG. PR0 drains inline hex in favor of tokens; these greys are off-palette one-offs that won't re-theme with --accent and read as arbitrary.
findings.fix: Use currentColor + a Tailwind text color (e.g. wrap in `text-brand-400`/`text-brand-600` and set stroke="currentColor"/fill="currentColor"), or point the trend line at var(--accent) so the hero's price trend matches the accent the figures now use.

findings.issue: The .count-tile dt rule (used by the hero knowledge tiles) hardcodes font-size:0.62rem (~9.9px) AND color:#838B9B. That violates the PR0 11px readability floor for the tile labels (Offers/Won/Sightings/Reqs) and uses an inline hex instead of a token. Because it lives in the shared CSS it silently affects the dossier hero.
findings.fix: Bump font-size to 0.6875rem (11px) to meet the floor and replace `color:#838B9B` with a token-backed grey (e.g. `color: var(--line)` is wrong semantically — use the brand-400/gray-500 utility on the element, or define the color via @apply text-gray-500). Smallest change: set font-size:0.6875rem and swap the hex for `@apply text-gray-500`.

findings.issue: Two text-[9px] instances fall under the PR0 11px readability floor: the buyer-avatar initials (hero line 116) and the spec evidence-tier 'Direct/Sourced' tag (specs line 32). The evidence tag in particular conveys provenance/trust and is barely legible.
findings.fix: Hero avatars: bump to text-[10px] minimum given the 20px circle, or accept as a decorative non-text-content exception (initials are redundant with the title attr) — at minimum raise to text-[10px]. Specs evidence tag (line 32): raise to text-[11px] (it is meaningful body content) and reuse `.badge-mini` / `.chip` rather than the one-off rounded px-1 span.

findings.issue: Every secondary/outline button (Add Offer, Add to Requisition, rail Add Offer/Add to Req, mobile Actions) re-implements the same outline pattern by hand: `bg-white border border-brand-300 text-brand-700 ... hover:bg-brand-50`. PR0 provides .btn-secondary as the locked outline primitive. The hand-rolled variants drift in padding (px-4 py-2 vs px-3 py-1.5 vs px-3 py-2.5) and are line-debt-adjacent.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke outline buttons with `.btn-secondary` (+ `.btn-sm` where a compact size is wanted). One primitive normalizes padding/border/hover across the hero bar, the rail, the mobile sheet, and the drawer, and kills the brand-300 hairline borders in the same stroke.

findings.issue: shortlist_bar's 'Create RFQ' uses a hand-rolled outline (px-4 py-2 ... bg-white text-brand-700 border border-brand-300) while its sibling 'Add to Requisition' correctly uses .btn-primary (line 22). Two buttons in the same bar, one primitive and one bespoke — inconsistent button vocabulary in a single component.
findings.fix: Convert 'Create RFQ' to `.btn-secondary` and the trailing 'Clear' to `.btn-ghost btn-sm` so all three buttons in the bar use the locked .btn-* family.

findings.issue: Pervasive border-gray-* hairlines across the dossier (history_panel uses border-gray-50/100 on every accordion/row divider; vendor_card sub-offer table border-gray-100/200; the lead-drawer panel border-l border-gray-200; the history skeleton border-gray-100). PR0 introduced --line/--line-subtle via .border-line-* and is draining border-gray-* page by page. These hairlines currently lean on the legacy !important override block.
findings.fix: Sweep border-gray-100/200/50 → .border-line-subtle (and divide-gray-* → divide-[color:var(--line-subtle)] or the brand-100 dividers already used elsewhere on this page for consistency). The dossier already uses divide-brand-100 in the market list, so align the history panel/drawer to the same hairline.

findings.issue: The 'What we know' panel and lead-detail use blue-600/blue-50/blue-700 for the 'Open full part page →' / 'View full FRU matrix →' links and buyer chips, while the rest of the dossier (and PR0) standardize interactive accents on brand/accent. These blue-* links are a third color family competing with brand and accent in the same surface.
findings.fix: Recolor the inline link CTAs to `text-brand-600 hover:text-brand-700` (or accent via a small link utility) and the buyer/cross-ref chips to bg-brand-100 text-brand-700 to match the hero's brand pills, removing the stray blue-* family.

findings.issue: The expandable sub-offers table is a bespoke <table class="w-full text-xs"> with hand-inlined `py-1` cells and a gray-500 thead, re-implementing a table primitive instead of using .compact-table / the named .compact-cell utilities the foundation provides. Header is text-gray-500 (not the unified 600/0.05em/11px uppercase header spec).
findings.fix: Apply `compact-table` to the <table> (gets the locked th/td padding + unified header type) or, if keeping it inline, swap `py-1` cells for `.compact-cell--dense` and the thead row for the 11px-uppercase header treatment so the nested table matches the app's table density.

findings.issue: The drawer's evidence section uses a near-black bg-gray-800 / border-gray-700 'terminal' block — an off-palette one-off dark card that exists nowhere else in the foundation and uses border-gray-700 hairlines. It reads as a bespoke island vs the white .card surfaces around it.
findings.fix: Either bring it into the palette (bg-brand-900 with border-line-base, if a dark evidence block is intentional) or convert it to a normal white .card with the same data — and replace border-gray-700 with a token. Low priority but it's the most visually divergent block on the surface.

findings.issue: Per-row market actions (RFQ this / +Offer / Details) are lg:opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 — fully hidden until hover on desktop. 'The phone is the weapon': the next outreach action should be ~one click away and discoverable. Hover-to-reveal hides the fastest path to action and is invisible to keyboard/touch-pad users who don't hover, weakening the Three-Second 'what do I do next'.
findings.fix: Make the row actions persistently visible at low emphasis (e.g. lg:opacity-60 group-hover:opacity-100) so the RFQ/Details affordance is always discoverable, or at minimum reveal on focus-within (add `lg:focus-within:opacity-100`) so keyboard tabbing exposes them.

findings.issue: Minor loading-to-content rhythm gap: the shell market skeleton (lines 84-89) renders rows with `px-4 py-3` and a left bar, but the real cache-hit market (dossier_market.html) leads with a `px-4 py-2.5` freshness header before the rows — so on swap the content shifts vs the skeleton. Small but visible jump on every dossier load.
findings.fix: Add a matching ~py-2.5 header placeholder bar to the skeleton (a thin freshness/refresh row) so the skeleton geometry matches the resolved market header and the swap doesn't reflow.

topOpportunities: One sweep to migrate every primary CTA to .btn-primary and the search inputs to .input/.input-focus (dossier_hero.html:134, dossier_shell.html:26/42/171, lead_detail.html:191, form.html:16, dossier_shell.html:22). This is the single highest-leverage fix: it moves the page's most-used controls onto the Trio-azure accent and the accent focus ring in one pass, fixes the missing keyboard focus ring on the tier-1 search bar, and deletes a pile of duplicated padding/color classes — pure conformance win with near-zero risk.

Emphasize the decision figures: add .figure-accent to the hero market price and the four knowledge-count numerals (dossier_hero.html:79,108-111). These are the numbers a buyer triages by in the first 3 seconds; accent + tabular-nums makes the dossier's value proposition (price + institutional memory) pop instead of reading as flat brand-900 text.

Hairline + stray-color sweep: convert the border-gray-* dividers in history_panel/vendor_card/drawer to .border-line-subtle (align to the divide-brand-100 already used in the market list) and recolor the blue-600 'Open full part page'/'FRU matrix' links and buyer chips to brand. This drains this page off the legacy border-gray !important block and removes the third (blue) color family competing with brand/accent.

Make per-vendor row actions discoverable (vendor_card.html:67): the live-market list IS the page's action surface, but RFQ/+Offer/Details are hover-only on desktop and invisible to keyboard/touch users. Showing them at low emphasis (or on focus-within) directly serves 'the phone is the weapon' — the next outreach action one click away — for the highest-traffic interaction on the dossier.

perPage.summary: The Buy Plans deal hub (hub.html → _board/_orders_queue/_supervise lenses, plus detail.html) is functionally rich and role-aware, and it scores well on the Three-Second Rule: clear `<h1>`, status badge, progress stepper, slim metric strips, and prominent role-aware action banners. But it predates the PR0 foundation and almost entirely re-implements primitives by hand: both tables are bespoke `min-w-full divide-y` markup (not data-table/compact-table) with inlined `px-4 py-3` / `text-xs text-gray-500` headers, so they render looser and lighter-weight than the rest of the app; the active lens pill, inline-form focus rings, and several CTAs still use brand-* where the foundation now wants accent; and hairlines are border-gray-* rather than .border-line-*. The detail page is also internally inconsistent — some buttons are `.btn btn-primary btn-sm`, others are raw `bg-emerald-600`/`bg-rose-600`. Nothing is broken, but the page is one of the higher-debt surfaces against the new baseline.

findings.issue: Both data tables (orders queue + line items) are hand-rolled `<table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200">` with inline `px-4 py-3` cells and `text-xs font-medium text-gray-500` headers. This ignores the new density locks: data-table td is now 5px 10px and th is 7px 10px, and headers are unified to 11px/600/0.05em uppercase #6B7280. The page reads measurably looser and its headers lighter (font-medium vs 600) than every other table in the app.
findings.fix: Convert both tables to `class="data-table"` (or `compact-table` for the dense line grid) and drop the per-cell `px-4 py-3` and per-th `text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 uppercase tracking-wider` — the class supplies padding, weight, tracking, and the 2px header rule. Keep only alignment utilities (text-right) on cells.

findings.issue: The active lens pill ('My Deals'/'My Orders'/'Supervise') resolves to `bg-brand-500 text-white` for the selected state. Per PR0 the single accent (Trio azure) now owns active-state chrome; an active selector on brand-500 is foundation-debt. (The resell sibling shares this exact pattern, so it is a program-wide sweep, but it is still debt here.)
findings.fix: Change the active branch to the accent: `'bg-accent-600 text-white shadow-sm'` (or a shared `.tab-pill--active` if one is introduced), leaving the inactive `bg-brand-100 text-brand-600` neutral chrome as-is.

findings.issue: Every inline form input/textarea uses `border border-brand-200 ... focus:ring-brand-500` (or `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500`). The foundation centralizes input styling in `.input`/`.input-sm` whose focus ring resolves to --accent. A brand-500 focus ring on a primary data-entry control is exactly the focus-ring case PR0 moved to accent.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke input classes with `.input` (or `.input-sm` for the w-28 PO# fields), which supplies the border, padding, and accent focus ring. This also drops the per-field `border-brand-200 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500` repetition.

findings.issue: Action buttons are inconsistent: some use the shared `.btn btn-primary btn-sm` (lines 195,217,233,534), but Approve/Reject/Verify/Confirm are hand-rolled `px-3 py-1.5 ... bg-emerald-600/bg-rose-600 text-white rounded-md`. Two button systems coexist on the same screen, and the approve/reject greens/roses don't map to any btn variant. This is bespoke markup re-implementing the .btn primitive.
findings.fix: Standardize: destructive actions → `.btn btn-danger btn-sm`; approve/verify → a `.btn-success` variant (add one to styles.css mirroring btn-danger) or `.btn btn-primary btn-sm` if approve should read as the primary accent action. Remove the raw `bg-emerald-600/bg-rose-600 px-… py-…` button markup.

findings.issue: Card and section hairlines use `border-gray-200`/`border-gray-100` (column shells, triage card headers, team-orders table). PR0 introduced --line/--line-subtle via .border-line-base/.border-line-subtle and is draining border-gray-* page by page; these are line-debt.
findings.fix: Swap `border-gray-200` → `.border-line-base` and the lighter internal dividers `border-gray-100` → `.border-line-subtle` on the card/section/column borders. Leave the brand-200 card borders (which already match the card primitive) alone.

findings.issue: On the detail header the two hero figures a buyer scans for — total value (`$…`) and margin % — are rendered as plain `text-sm text-gray-900 font-semibold`, the same weight/size as the 'N lines / N vendors' metadata beside them. The most important numbers on the deal don't stand out, weakening the 'what needs my attention' read.
findings.fix: Emphasize the total value with `.figure-accent` (the opt-in hero-numeric accent) and/or bump to `text-base`/`text-lg`, and keep the margin in its semantic emerald/amber/rose. Demote 'lines/vendors' to a lighter weight so the money figure wins the hierarchy.

findings.issue: Several status/metadata pills are hand-rolled `inline-flex px-… py-… text-xs … rounded-full` (Stock Sale chip, SO 'pending' pill, queue 'PO to cut'/'verifying' badges) rather than the shared `.badge`/`.chip` primitives that status_badge already emits elsewhere on this same page. Bespoke pills drift from the badge family's padding/border.
findings.fix: Route these through `.badge` (status/lifecycle) or `.chip` (the 'stock' metadata tag), e.g. `<span class="badge bg-amber-50 text-amber-700 border-amber-200">`, so they inherit the locked badge geometry. The line_colors/so_colors maps already feed status_badge — extend that pattern instead of inlining.

findings.issue: The lazy-body loading placeholder is a bare `p-8 text-center text-gray-400 text-sm` 'Loading…' with no skeleton or spinner — and the surrounding lens chrome paints first, so the body flashes empty. Minor, but it's the first thing a buyer sees on every hub visit.
findings.fix: Use the shared loading affordance (spinner/skeleton) consistent with other lazy bodies, or at minimum tighten `p-8` to match the page's p-3 rhythm and add the app spinner icon so the empty flash reads as intentional loading.

findings.issue: Deal cards and triage rows are clickable via `hx-get` on a `<div>`/`<a>` with `cursor-pointer` but no `tabindex`/`role=link` and no visible focus state, so keyboard users can't reach or see focus on the primary navigation target of the board. Contrast: PR0 routes focus rings through accent on real controls.
findings.fix: Make card/row navigations real focusable controls — either an `<a href>`-style element with the accent focus ring, or add `tabindex="0" role="link"` plus a `focus-visible:ring-2` accent ring and an Enter/Space handler — so the board is keyboard-navigable per the 'fast, keyboard-navigable' doctrine.

topOpportunities: Convert the two bespoke tables (orders-queue + line-items) to the shared data-table/compact-table classes. This is the single highest-leverage move: it instantly fixes the density-debt (5px/10px locks), unifies header type (11px/600/0.05em), and removes ~40 inlined `px-4 py-3 text-xs font-medium text-gray-500` declarations — bringing the buyer's two most-used grids into visual parity with the rest of the app.

Sweep brand-* → accent on active/interactive states: the lens pill active branch (hub.html) and the inline-form focus rings (→ `.input`/`.input-sm`). Small, mechanical, and it makes the page obey the one-accent rule where a buyer's eye actually tracks state.

Standardize the detail/supervise action buttons onto the .btn family (add a btn-success variant for Approve/Verify; btn-danger for Reject). Eliminates the dual button system on the most action-dense screen and makes the primary CTA reliably read as the accent.

Strengthen the detail-header hierarchy by promoting the total-value figure with .figure-accent and de-emphasizing the lines/vendors metadata, so the 'what's this deal worth / what needs attention' answer lands in under three seconds.

findings.issue: The lens switch ('My Lists' / 'Open to Me') is the primary mode toggle on this page, and its ACTIVE state hard-codes `bg-brand-500 text-white`. Under PR0 the one accent for active states is Trio azure (accent-*); this active pill still reads as the old brand blue, so it diverges from the bottom-nav active indicator and the accent CTAs sitting right next to it.
findings.fix: Swap the active branch to the accent family: `bg-accent-500 text-white shadow-sm` (and `bg-accent-100 text-accent-700 hover:bg-accent-200` for the inactive branch to keep the pair on one ramp). Note this is a hand-rolled copy of `filter_pill` — see the consistency finding to collapse it to the macro once the macro itself is moved to accent.
findings.fix_short: Move the active lens pill from brand-500 to accent-500

findings.issue: The detail tab bar (Lines / Offers / Build Bid / Outreach / Activity) marks the ACTIVE tab with `border-brand-500 text-brand-700`. This is the page's primary in-panel navigation; per PR0 an active-state indicator must resolve to the accent, not brand-*. As-is the active tab underline is a different blue from the page loading bar and active bottom-nav.
findings.fix: Change the active branch to `border-accent-500 text-accent-700`. Leaves the inactive/hover branch (`border-transparent text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700`) untouched.
findings.fix_short: Active tab underline/label to accent-* instead of brand-*

findings.issue: The triage stat-card 'selected' affordance uses `ring-2 ring-brand-400`. The triage strip is the page's primary attention/urgency cue (the Three-Second-Rule 'what needs my attention' surface), so its selected ring is an active-state indicator and should carry the accent under PR0.
findings.fix: Change to `ring-2 ring-accent-400` so the selected triage card matches the lens pill and tab accent.
findings.fix_short: Triage selected ring brand-400 to accent-400

findings.issue: The list-search input is a bespoke input with `focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500` instead of the foundation `.input`/`.input-focus` classes. PR0 routes every focus ring through `--accent-ring`; this one still glows brand blue, and it also re-implements `.input`'s padding/border by hand (px-3 py-1.5, border-brand-200).
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke classes with the `.input` class (it already carries the accent focus ring, rounded-lg, and tight padding): `class="input"` plus `type="search"`. If the brand-200 border tint is intentional, keep only that one override; the ring must come from .input.
findings.fix_short: List search to .input (accent focus ring) not focus:ring-brand-500

findings.issue: The buyer-search typeahead in the 'Offer to buyers' modal hand-rolls a focus ring with `focus:border-brand-400 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-200` instead of using `.input`. Focus rings are an explicit PR0 accent surface; this one is brand-tinted and a second smaller ring radius than the rest of the app.
findings.fix: Replace with `class="input"` (drop the inline px/border/ring classes). Keeps the typeahead behavior; just unifies the focus treatment to the accent ring.

findings.issue: Three separate scope/channel segmented toggles ('whole_list'/'per_line', and the channel buttons) mark the ACTIVE segment with `bg-brand-500 text-white` (line 162, 165, 178). Same pattern repeats in offer_form.html:25,28. These are active-state selectors and should be on the accent ramp per PR0; brand-500 here reads inconsistently against the accent primary CTA in the same modal footer.
findings.fix: Replace the active branches `bg-brand-500 text-white` (and `border-brand-500` on 178) with `bg-accent-500 text-white` / `border-accent-500`, and the inactive hover `hover:bg-brand-50` with `hover:bg-accent-50`. Do the same two edits in offer_form.html:25,28.
findings.fix_short: Segmented-toggle active state brand-500 to accent-500 (both modals)

findings.issue: Opportunity-row dividers use `border-b border-gray-100`. Per PR0 hairlines should use the line tokens (.border-line-base / .border-line-subtle) rather than border-gray-*, which is the legacy override block being drained page-by-page. Same border-gray-100 hairline recurs on the modal footers (offer_form.html:74, create_modal.html:32, add_line_modal.html:47, offer_buyers_modal.html:204).
findings.fix: Change the list-row divider to `border-b border-line-subtle`. For the four modal footers, the shared `.modal-footer` class already encapsulates the bordered footer (border-brand-200) — fold those bespoke `pt-2 border-t border-gray-100` footers into `.modal-footer` to both fix the line token and adopt the locked modal padding.

findings.issue: The lens switch re-implements the `filter_pill` macro inline (same `px-3.5 py-1.5 rounded-full font-medium` pill, same active/inactive brand classes) rather than calling the shared macro — a primitive re-implemented by hand. The page already imports and uses filter_pill correctly in _lists.html:25, so this is an inconsistency within the same feature. (The hand-roll exists because the lens needs Alpine `:class` reactivity the macro doesn't expose.)
findings.fix: Either extend `filter_pill` to accept an optional Alpine `:class` reactive variant, or at minimum align the hand-rolled pill's classes to the macro's exact spec (`px-3 py-1.5`, accent active branch) so the two pill rows on the page are visually identical. Lowest-effort: keep the hand-roll but match classes + move to accent.
findings.fix_short: Align hand-rolled lens pills to filter_pill spec/accent

findings.issue: Best-offer unit price — the single most decision-relevant figure for a trader scanning a list — is rendered as `text-emerald-600` body weight in the table, while the take-all lump price in _offers.html:48 gets `text-lg font-bold`. The key figure on the default (Lines) tab is not emphasized, weakening the Three-Second-Rule 'what should I do next' read. PR0 provides `.figure-accent` for hero numerics; emerald is fine as the 'good price' signal but the figure has no size/weight hierarchy over the qty/condition cells next to it.
findings.fix: Keep emerald for the 'best price' semantic but give the figure weight hierarchy — it already has font-semibold; consider promoting the single-line card's Best-offer (_lines.html:90) to the same prominence as the take-all lump (text-lg). No token change required, this is a weight/scale pass so the buyer's eye lands on price first.
findings.fix_short: Emphasize best-offer price as the key figure on Lines tab

findings.issue: The Activity tab is a permanent 'coming soon' placeholder shipped as a real tab in the bar. A buyer who clicks it hits a dead end, costing a click against the Three-Second-Rule and implying institutional-memory (last-contacted, part history, prior outcomes) that the page promises elsewhere but doesn't deliver here.
findings.fix: Either hide the Activity tab until the timeline ships (remove the `tabs + [('activity','Activity')]` append on detail.html:111) or wire it to the existing activity-timeline partial used elsewhere in the app. Hiding is the S-effort honest move; wiring is M.
findings.fix_short: Hide or wire the placeholder Activity tab

findings.issue: The draft import dropzone uses a bespoke `rounded-lg border border-dashed border-brand-200 bg-gray-50 p-3` block rather than the shared empty-state/dropzone vocabulary. Minor, but it is a one-off surface that another page's importer would render differently.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is given there's no shared dropzone primitive, but the `border-brand-200` dashed border could move to `border-line-base` for hairline consistency. Low priority — flag only.
findings.fix_short: Dropzone dashed border to line token (optional)

findings.issue: The detail tab buttons are `<button>`s toggling Alpine `tab` state but expose no `role="tab"`/`aria-selected`/`aria-controls`, and the tab-panel divs have no `role="tabpanel"`. Keyboard users get focusable buttons (good) but no screen-reader tab semantics. The offer-count badge (`{{ offer_count }}`) also has no accessible label distinguishing it from the tab name.
findings.fix: Add `role="tab"` + `:aria-selected="tab === '{{ key }}'"` to each tab button and `role="tabpanel"` to the x-show panels; wrap the count badge with `aria-label="{{ offer_count }} offers"`. Pairs naturally with the accent active-state edit on the same line.
findings.fix_short: Add ARIA tab/tabpanel roles to detail tabs

findings.issue: Loading and empty placeholders use raw `text-gray-400`/`text-gray-300` ad-hoc markup rather than a shared empty-state/skeleton primitive, and the 'Loading…' string differs from the lazy-tab 'Loading offers…' / 'Loading bid builder…' phrasings — minor inconsistency in the loading vocabulary across the same page.
findings.fix: Low priority. If a shared `empty_state`/skeleton macro exists, route the right-panel empty (lines 108-114) and the list-loading stub through it; otherwise standardize the loading copy. Flag only.

perPage.summary: The Resell workspace is one of the cleaner pages in the app: it leans hard on the shared vocabulary (compact-table, filter_pill, stat_card, status_badge, coverage_meter, .btn-primary/.btn-secondary, .card, .input), the split-panel matches the sourcing workspace, lazy-loads carry their explicit hx-target (avoiding the documented landmine), and empty states are honest invitations rather than blank tables. The dominant gap is PR0 accent adoption: every active-state indicator on the page — the lens switch, the detail tab bar, the triage selected-ring, the offer/channel segmented toggles — and every bespoke focus ring still resolve to brand-500/700/400 rather than the new accent-* family, so the page's blues are subtly inconsistent with the accent CTAs and bottom-nav sitting right beside them. Secondary debt is a handful of border-gray-100 hairlines (rows + four modal footers) that should move to line tokens / the .modal-footer class, two inputs that hand-roll what .input now provides, and a permanent 'coming soon' Activity tab that dead-ends.
topOpportunities: Sweep all active-state indicators to the accent ramp in one pass: lens switch (workspace.html:28), detail tab bar (detail.html:114), triage ring (workspace.html:67), and the four segmented toggles across offer_buyers_modal.html + offer_form.html. This is the single highest-leverage change — it's ~8 one-line edits that bring the page's entire interactive-blue surface onto the PR0 accent, eliminating the brand-vs-accent flicker against the primary CTAs and bottom-nav.

Unify the two bespoke inputs (_lists.html:38 search, offer_buyers_modal.html:135 typeahead) onto the `.input` class so their focus rings flow through --accent-ring instead of brand tints — removes hand-rolled padding/border/ring duplication and fixes the focus-ring accent debt in the same edit.

Convert the border-gray-100 hairlines to line tokens — fold the four modal footers (offer_form/create/add_line/offer_buyers) into the shared `.modal-footer` (gets both the line token AND the locked padding) and change the list-row divider to `.border-line-subtle`. Drains this page from the legacy border-gray override block.

Make a decision on the Activity tab: either hide it (S) or wire it to the existing activity-timeline partial (M). A permanent 'coming soon' tab is a Three-Second-Rule dead-end and undercuts the page's institutional-memory promise.

perPage.summary: The CRM route is a thin Alpine tab shell (shell.html) over two very different surfaces: a modern split-panel Customers/CDM workspace (list.html + _account_list.html) and a much looser, pre-foundation Vendors table (vendors/list.html). The Customers side is dense and well-structured but predates PR0: it still uses brand-500/600 for active/selected states, inlines focus rings (focus:ring-brand-500), and hand-rolls cell padding rather than using accent-*, .input/.input-focus, or the named cell utilities. The Vendors tab is the bigger debt sink — a bespoke px-4 py-3 table with font-medium gray-500 headers (vs the locked .data-table th 600/11px/7px-10px), text-brand-500 as the primary link/title color, and border-gray-200 hairlines, so it reads noticeably looser and off-accent next to the rest of the app. Neither tab uses the new accent for primary CTAs/active state, and the shell's own tab bar is still entirely brand-500/600.

findings.issue: The CRM tab bar's active state is brand-500/600 (border-b-2 border-brand-500 text-brand-600 bg-brand-50). Per PR0 the active tab indicator/label is an ACTIVE state and must resolve to the accent, not brand-*. This is the very first thing a buyer sees on /v2/crm and it's off-foundation.
findings.fix: Swap the active branch to accent: border-accent-500 text-accent-700 (or text-accent-600) and bg-accent-50; keep the inactive hover on neutral grays. Mirror whatever sibling tab bars adopted in PR0 so all tab bars match.

findings.issue: Vendors sub-tab bar (All Vendors / My Vendors / Find by Part) uses border-gray-200 underline and text-brand-600 border-brand-500 for the active tab. Active state should be accent-*, and the hairline should be a line token. This diverges from both the foundation and the CRM shell's own tab bar above it.
findings.fix: Active branch → text-accent-700 border-accent-500; container border-b-2 → border-line-base (or border-accent style matching the shell). Ideally factor these two tab bars onto the shared tab_button macro so CRM has one tab vocabulary.

findings.issue: Vendor table is hand-rolled: <table class="min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200"> with <th class="px-4 py-3 ... font-medium text-gray-500">. The locked .data-table th is font-weight 600 / 11px / letter-spacing .05em / padding 7px 10px, and td is 5px 10px. This table is both looser (px-4 py-3) and lighter-weight (font-medium 500) than every other table in the app, so the Vendors tab visibly reads as a different era.
findings.fix: Convert to the shared table primitive: wrap in .table-wrapper and use class="data-table" on the <table>, dropping the inline px-4 py-3 / divide-y / font-medium / text-gray-500 on th and td. Header cells then inherit the unified 600/11px/uppercase spec and the locked padding automatically.

findings.issue: Vendor display name (the primary clickable identity / key figure in each row) is text-brand-500. Under PR0 the accent is the one azure for primary emphasis; a key linked figure should be accent-*, and brand-500 as a 'link blue' is exactly the legacy usage being drained. Same brand-500 link color recurs in the sort-header hover (line 165 hover:text-brand-500).
findings.fix: Change the vendor name to text-accent-700 (or accent-600) and the sort-header hover to hover:text-accent-600. Reserve brand-* for neutral chrome only.

findings.issue: Selected-row highlight is brand-50 + border-l-brand-500 (selectedId === c.id ? 'bg-brand-50 border-l-brand-500'). Row selection is an active state and should carry the accent so the chosen account matches the app-wide selection color introduced in PR0.
findings.fix: Swap to bg-accent-50 border-l-accent-500 for the selected branch. (The selected-row treatment in the opportunity table / sightings should be the reference.)

findings.issue: Every filter/sort control in the CDM filter bar inlines its own field styling — 'px-3 py-1.5 text-sm border border-gray-200 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500 focus:border-brand-500' on the search input and 'px-2 py-1.5 ... border-gray-200' on each <select>. PR0 provides .input/.input-sm (accent ring + line border) precisely so controls stop hand-rolling brand-500 rings and gray borders.
findings.fix: Replace the search input class with .input (or .input .input-sm) and the selects with .input .input-sm, dropping the focus:ring-brand-500/border-gray-200 inline classes. Checkboxes (lines 75,88,113,123) should use .input-focus + text-accent-500 instead of text-brand-500 focus:ring-brand-500.

findings.issue: Vendor list re-implements card/wrapper chrome three times as 'bg-white rounded-lg shadow border border-gray-200' (filter bar, table wrapper, empty state) instead of the .card / .table-wrapper primitives. rounded-lg + shadow also diverges from .card's rounded-xl + shadow-card, so these surfaces don't match the Customers side or the rest of the app.
findings.fix: Use .card for the filter-bar and empty-state panels and .table-wrapper for the table container. This also fixes the border-gray-200 hairline debt on those three blocks in one move.

findings.issue: 'Add Vendor' primary CTA uses bare class="btn-primary btn-sm" with no .btn base — and elsewhere the codebase composes .btn-primary which already @applies btn. More notably it sits visually as the page's primary action but the whole Vendors header has no title/eyebrow (only '{{total}} vendors'), so the primary CTA floats without a 'where am I' anchor (Three-Second Rule).
findings.fix: Keep class="btn-primary btn-sm" (btn-primary already includes btn, so this is fine — but verify it renders the accent fill). Add a lightweight section heading/eyebrow (e.g. an .h2 'Vendors' or reuse the shell tab as the title) so the count and CTA have a titled header.

findings.issue: The Import-CSV modal in the Customers list is hand-rolled (fixed inset-0 bg-black/40, bg-white rounded-xl p-6, manual × close, ad-hoc header) instead of the locked .modal-header/.modal-body/.modal-footer/.modal-close primitives that the sibling Vendors import modal (vendors/list.html:39-58) correctly uses. Two CRM modals, two different modal vocabularies.
findings.fix: Rebuild the customers import modal on the shared modal-* chrome (modal-header with .h4 title + .modal-close, modal-body, modal-footer with btn-secondary/btn-primary) to match the vendors import modal and the rest of the app.

findings.issue: Three-Second Rule, 'Where am I?': the CRM shell has no page title/breadcrumb and no urgency cue at the shell level. It opens straight into a tab bar + a generic spinner. The 'X need a call' urgency count exists but is buried inside the Customers account-list (only visible after the lazy load and only if overdue_count>0). A buyer landing on /v2/crm sees no attention signal until the inner partial paints.
findings.fix: Add a slim CRM header above the tab bar (title 'CRM' / 'Companies & Vendors') and surface the call-list urgency badge ('N accounts need a call') at the shell or tab-label level so attention is answerable before the inner partial loads.

findings.issue: Win rate, Rev 90d, and Last req are the account's key commercial figures but are rendered as font-semibold text-gray-900 inline in a dot-separated chip strip, with no accent emphasis. PR0 added .figure-accent specifically for hero numerics (deal value / margin / win%); these read as ordinary metadata rather than the institutional-memory signal a buyer scans for.
findings.fix: Apply .figure-accent to the Win % and Rev 90d values (the two decision-driving figures) so they pop as key figures, consistent with how hero numerics are treated elsewhere post-PR0.

findings.issue: Row and pagination focus rings are inlined as focus:ring-brand-500 (and focus:ring-inset focus:ring-brand-500 on the clickable rows). These are real, keyboard-reachable focus targets, so post-PR0 the focus ring should be the accent ring, not brand-500. Functionally present, but off the new focus color.
findings.fix: Replace focus:ring-brand-500 with the accent focus treatment (focus:ring-accent-500, or add the .input-focus mixin where it composes). Do this consistently across the row, the resize divider (list.html:183 hover:bg-brand-400), and pagination links.

findings.issue: The shell's only loading state is a centered gray spinner + 'Loading...' for the entire tab content. Both the Customers split-panel and the Vendors table are content-rich; a bare spinner gives no shape of what's coming and flashes a layout jump when the dense partial swaps in. The inner empty states (vendors 'No vendors found', accounts 'No accounts found') are fine, but the shell-level loader is the weakest moment.
findings.fix: Lower-priority, but consider a skeleton matching the split-panel/table shape (or at minimum reuse the app's standard loading partial) so the swap doesn't jump. Not blocking — note it as polish.

topOpportunities: Adopt the foundation on the Vendors tab — it's the single biggest visual outlier in CRM: convert the hand-rolled px-4 py-3 / font-medium gray table to .table-wrapper + .data-table, move text-brand-500 name/links and the active sub-tab to accent-*, and replace the three 'bg-white rounded-lg shadow border-gray-200' blocks with .card/.table-wrapper. One template, removes most of this page's foundation debt and makes Vendors match Customers.

Move all active/selected/focus states across both CRM surfaces to the accent: shell tab bar (_shell_macros.html active branch), selected account row (_account_list.html:111 bg-brand-50 border-l-brand-500), and the inline focus:ring-brand-500 rings. High-visibility, low-effort, and makes CRM read as 'on the new foundation' instantly.

Give the CRM shell a Three-Second-Rule header: a title/breadcrumb plus the call-list urgency count surfaced at the shell/tab level instead of buried inside the lazy-loaded account list, so a buyer landing on /v2/crm immediately sees where they are and what needs a call.

Unify the two import modals on the shared modal-* primitives (rebuild the customers list.html modal to match the vendors one) and swap the CDM filter-bar controls to .input/.input-sm — both eliminate hand-rolled brand-500 rings and gray borders and align CRM's form chrome with the rest of the app.

perPage.summary: The Proactive page is a well-structured, genuinely useful surface (grouped matches, trust cues like "trusted"/"unreliable", purchase-count memory, prepare/send/convert workflow) but it predates the PR0 foundation almost entirely. Every interactive accent still resolves to brand-* (active tab, all primary CTAs, focus rings, scorecard figures) instead of the new Trio-azure accent; the one place it does adopt the new vocabulary is the empty-state "Check again" .btn-primary, proving the convention is available and just not applied elsewhere. The match/sent/prepare tables hand-roll min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200 with inline px-3 py-2.5 / px-4 py-3 cell padding and border-gray-* headers rather than .data-table/.compact-table + the named cell/line utilities, so the page reads looser and lighter-lined than the rest of the app. There is also no page <h1> at all, weakening the "Where am I?" answer.

findings.issue: The active tab indicator uses bg-brand-500 text-white shadow-sm and inactive tabs use bg-brand-50 text-brand-600. Active/selected state is exactly what PR0 moves to the accent. Both Matches and Sent tabs are brand-* on the most important 'what am I looking at' control.
findings.fix: Active tab: background-color:var(--accent); color:var(--accent-contrast) (or a bg-accent-500 text-white utility). Inactive: keep neutral chrome but make the active label/indicator accent. Mirror however the active bottom-nav indicator now resolves so the in-page tab matches the global active treatment.

findings.issue: The primary 'Prepare (N)' CTA is hand-rolled bg-brand-500 hover:bg-brand-600 rounded-lg rather than .btn-primary, so it does not resolve to the accent and re-implements a primitive. This is the page's main forward action.
findings.fix: Replace the bespoke classes with .btn-primary .btn-sm (the empty-state 'Check again' button on line 184 already uses .btn-primary — match it). Keep the :disabled binding.

findings.issue: The 'Send to N Contacts' submit — the terminal primary action of the whole proactive workflow — is hand-rolled bg-brand-500 hover:bg-brand-600 instead of the accent .btn-primary.
findings.fix: Use .btn-primary (with the existing :disabled='contactCount === 0' binding). Same for the brand-500 Prepare button; converge all primary CTAs in the cluster on .btn-primary so they all read Trio azure.

findings.issue: The 'Scorecard' toggle and 'Generate AI Draft' button use text-brand-600 bg-brand-50 border-brand-200 hover:bg-brand-100 — a brand-tinted secondary treatment. With the accent now the single interactive color, these brand-tinted action buttons diverge from the new vocabulary.
findings.fix: Move to a shared secondary/ghost primitive (.btn-secondary or .btn-ghost) for neutral chrome, or to an accent-tinted variant (accent-50/accent-200/accent-700) so the tint matches the new accent rather than legacy brand.

findings.issue: Every checkbox uses text-brand-500 focus:ring-brand-500 for the checked color and focus ring. Focus rings are explicitly part of the PR0 accent migration (.input-focus resolves to --accent-ring); these still ring brand.
findings.fix: Apply .input-focus to the native checkboxes (it sets the accent ring) and switch the checked accent to text-accent-500 (or accent-color:var(--accent)). Covers row selection, select-all, and the contact picker.

findings.issue: The sell-price number input uses inline border-gray-300 focus:border-brand-500 focus:ring-brand-500 with hand-rolled py-1 px-2 instead of the .input/.input-sm primitive, so its focus ring is brand (not accent) and its padding/border diverge from every other field on the page (the subject/body inputs already use class='input').
findings.fix: Replace with class='input input-sm w-24 text-right tabular-nums' so the focus ring resolves to the accent and the control matches the subject/body fields (lines 171,179).

findings.issue: The scorecard's hero numerics (Sent, Conv. Rate, Revenue, Gross Profit, Pipeline) are text-2xl font-bold in text-gray-900 / text-brand-700 / text-emerald-700 / text-amber-700. These are the page's key figures — exactly what .figure-accent (opt-in accent for hero numerics) targets — yet none use it, and the brand-700 Conv. Rate figure is foundation-debt.
findings.fix: Apply .figure-accent to the headline activity figure (e.g. Sent or Conv. Rate) so the primary KPI reads accent; keep emerald/amber for the explicitly semantic money tiles. At minimum convert the text-brand-700 Conv. Rate value off brand.

findings.issue: Group headers and all three tables draw hairlines with border-gray-200 / divide-gray-200 / divide-gray-100 instead of the --line tokens (line-debt the page-by-page sweep is meant to drain). The borders here are visibly lighter than .data-table's 1px #BFC4CE rows elsewhere.
findings.fix: Convert group-header borders to .border-line-base and, if tables are migrated to .data-table/.compact-table (see density finding), the row/header hairlines come from the table class for free. Otherwise swap divide-gray-* → the .border-line-* classes.

findings.issue: The Matches table is bespoke (min-w-full divide-y divide-gray-200) with every cell inlining px-3 py-2.5 and every header px-3 py-2 text-xs font-medium text-gray-500 — looser than and stylistically off from the new locks (.data-table td 5px 10px, th 11px/600/0.05em uppercase). Headers are font-medium not 600, and text-xs not the 11px header spec.
findings.fix: Migrate the table to class='data-table' (or compact-table) and drop the per-cell px/py; the foundation supplies the ~5px 10px td / 7px 10px th padding and the unified 600/0.05em/11px uppercase header type. If a class migration is too invasive, at minimum replace inline cell padding with .table-cell/.table-cell--head and set headers to the 11px/600 spec.
