findings.issue: The Sent-offers table and the prepare.html 'Selected Parts' table repeat the same hand-rolled min-w-full divide-y pattern with px-4 py-2 / px-4 py-3 cell padding and font-medium text-gray-500 headers — the looser px-4 py-3 spacing the foundation explicitly flags, duplicated across three tables.
findings.fix: Apply the same .data-table/.compact-table migration to both tables so all three proactive tables share one density and header type; remove the inline px-4 py-3 cells.

findings.issue:
The page header has no title — it is an empty
wrapping only a

AI-matched vendor stock...

. There is no

anywhere on the list page (grep confirms zero h1/h2). The 'Where am I?' answer relies entirely on the bottom-nav highlight; the page never names itself.
findings.fix: Add an <h1>/page-title (e.g. 'Proactive') above the description, matching the prepare.html h1 treatment (text-xl font-bold) and the title pattern used by sibling pages, so the page self-identifies.

findings.issue: Urgency/attention cue is weak and inconsistent: the Matches tab count badge is bg-emerald-500 (green = success, not attention), while the per-group count is a flat text-gray-400 'N matches'. A buyer scanning for 'what needs my attention' gets a green pill (reads as done/good) and muted gray per-customer urgency.
findings.fix: Make the new-match count read as attention (accent or amber pill via .badge), not emerald; consider promoting the per-group match count to a .badge so high-value customers stand out. Reserve emerald for converted/won states (as the Sent tab already does).

findings.issue: Status/score/margin pills and count badges are all hand-rolled inline-flex ... rounded-full/rounded rather than the locked .badge/.chip/.badge-mini primitives. The Sent status pill, the match-count pill, the Score pill and Margin pill each re-implement a primitive with slightly different padding (px-2 py-0.5 vs px-1.5 py-0.5), causing drift.
findings.fix: Route status pills through .badge (with the semantic color map), score/margin micro-pills through .badge-mini or .chip. This unifies padding/radius and lets the badge family carry future restyles.

findings.issue: Secondary buttons are bespoke: the Dismiss button is bg-white border-gray-200 hover:bg-gray-50 and the prepare Cancel is a text-gray-600 <a> styled as a button — neither uses .btn-secondary/.btn-ghost, so secondary actions look different from secondary actions on other pages.
findings.fix: Use .btn-secondary for Dismiss and .btn-ghost (or .btn-secondary) for Cancel so secondary actions are consistent app-wide and inherit the accent focus ring.

findings.issue: The tab bar is built from <a hx-get> elements with no role='tab'/aria-selected and no aria-label distinguishing the active tab; the active state is conveyed by color only. Keyboard/screen-reader users can't tell which tab is current beyond the visual fill.
findings.fix: Add role='tab' + aria-selected='true|false' (or aria-current='page') on the tab anchors so the active tab is announced; ensure focus-visible shows the accent ring.

findings.issue: The Sent tab 'Sent To' cell truncates recipient emails with |truncate(40) but offers no title/tooltip with the full list, so a buyer can't see who an offer actually went to without leaving the page — works against the 'phone is the weapon / contact one click away' principle.
findings.fix: Add title='{{ offer.recipient_emails | join(", ") }}' on the cell (or render as wrapping .chip tags) so the full recipient list is recoverable on hover.

topOpportunities: Accent migration sweep (S, high leverage): move the active tab indicator, the Prepare and Send primary CTAs, the scorecard hero figure, and all checkbox/input focus rings off brand-* onto the accent (.btn-primary / .figure-accent / .input-focus / accent-*). This single change snaps the whole page onto the new foundation, and the page already proves the pattern with its empty-state .btn-primary.

Table primitives migration (M): convert the three hand-rolled tables (Matches, Sent, Selected Parts) to .data-table/.compact-table + named cell utilities. Removes inline px-3/px-4 py-2.5/py-3 density-debt, fixes border-gray-* line-debt, and unifies the font-medium/text-xs headers to the locked 11px/600/0.05em uppercase spec in one move.

Add a real page title + sharpen the attention cue (S): give the list page an <h1> 'Proactive' so it answers 'Where am I?', and recolor the new-match count from emerald (reads as 'done') to an accent/amber attention pill so 'what needs my attention?' is answered at a glance — both core Three-Second-Rule wins for near-zero effort.

Converge pills and secondary buttons on the shared vocabulary (S): route status/score/margin pills through .badge/.badge-mini and Dismiss/Cancel through .btn-secondary/.btn-ghost, eliminating the bespoke padding drift and giving secondary actions the accent focus ring for free.

perPage.summary: The prospecting cluster (list.html, _card.html, stats.html, detail.html, add_result.html) predates the PR0 foundation and adopts none of it: every primary CTA, active filter pill, and focus ring still resolves to brand-500/600 instead of the new accent, and not a single shared primitive (.btn-primary, .input, .badge/.chip, .card, .empty-state) is used — all are re-implemented inline with hardcoded px/py. The grid/card content is genuinely strong on the philosophy axis (fit/readiness/AI-match scores, buyer-ready badges, priority reasons, contacts, similar wins, warm-intro and "Call now" KPIs deliver trust-at-a-glance and institutional memory), but the list view has no page title/breadcrumb, so the "Where am I?" leg of the three-second rule is weak, and the primary "phone is the weapon" action (call/email the contact) is buried two clicks deep with no contact info or outreach CTA on the card itself. Foundation-debt is the dominant theme: this is a clean-content page wearing the old chrome.

findings.issue: Every primary CTA (Add prospect, Add, Claim, Create Requisition) and the active filter pill use bg-brand-500 hover:bg-brand-600 instead of the new accent. Post-PR0 these primary/active surfaces must resolve to --accent (Trio azure). This is the single biggest foundation gap on the page — the page's most important buttons are the wrong color vs the rest of the app.
findings.fix: Replace the inline `px-3 py-1.5 bg-brand-500 text-white ... rounded-lg hover:bg-brand-600` button markup with `.btn-primary` (add `.btn-sm` where the compact card variant is wanted). For the active filter pill at list.html:75 swap `bg-brand-500 text-white` for the accent (`bg-accent-500` / accent var). Leave the green Create-Requisition button as-is (intentional semantic success).

findings.issue: The cluster re-implements four shared primitives by hand: search/domain/sort inputs inline `px-3 py-2 border border-brand-200 rounded-lg focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500` instead of `.input`; status/source/reason pills inline `inline-flex px-2 py-0.5 text-xs ... rounded-full` instead of `.badge`/`.chip`; cards inline `bg-white border border-brand-200 rounded-lg p-4` instead of `.card`; buttons inline padding instead of `.btn-*`. This is exactly the 'one-off bespoke markup re-implementing a primitive' smell, and it's why the focus rings are still brand not accent.
findings.fix: Migrate inputs to `.input` (focus ring becomes accent for free), status/reason/source pills to `.badge`/`.chip` with the semantic color map, card containers to `.card`/`.card-sm`, and buttons to `.btn-primary`/`.btn-secondary`. This collapses ~all the brand-500 focus/active debt and the bespoke padding in one pass.

findings.issue: Vertical rhythm is looser than the post-PR0 locks: stats KPI panel uses mb-5, the filter bar mb-6 and space-y-3, the stats cards p-4, and card grid gap-4 — all inherited from the old whitespace-heavy era. A Bloomberg-dense buyer pipeline should pack more cards above the fold; the stacked stats(mb-5) + count-line(mb-4) + filters(mb-6) burns ~3 rows of vertical space before any prospect is visible.
findings.fix: Tighten to the foundation scale: stats panel mb-3, filter block mb-4 with space-y-2, grid gap-3, and stats cards to `.card-sm` (p-2.5). Consider collapsing the standalone count line (list.html:21) into the filter row to recover a row.

findings.issue: The list view has no page title or breadcrumb — the first visible text is the '{{ total }} prospects' count. The 'Where am I?' leg of the three-second rule relies entirely on the bottom-nav active state. Sibling detail.html has an h1, but the primary working surface (the grid) does not, so a deep-linked/refreshed list lands with no on-page heading.
findings.fix: Add a compact header row above the count: an `.h1`/`.h2` 'Prospecting' title (with a one-line subtitle like 'Suggested accounts ranked by fit + buying signals'), and move the Add-prospect control into that header row so title + primary action share the top line.

findings.issue: 'The phone is still the weapon,' but the card surfaces only a contact COUNT ('3 contacts · 1 DM') — no name, no email/phone, and no outreach CTA. Acting on a buyer-ready prospect requires click → detail → scan contacts → click email: 3+ clicks. The card's quick-action row only offers Claim/Dismiss, not the next outreach step.
findings.fix: On buyer-ready / claimed cards, surface the top decision-maker (name + title) and a one-click mailto/copy-email or 'Email' action in the quick-action row, so the next outreach is ~one click from the grid. Reuse contact_stats_map's existing data; the detail page already has the contact list to model the row from.

findings.issue: All inputs and buttons hardcode `focus:ring-brand-500` (or have no focus style at all, e.g. the filter-pill buttons list.html:74 and card action buttons _card.html:123). Post-PR0 the canonical visible focus ring is the accent via `.input`/`.btn-*`. Keyboard users get an off-brand ring on inputs and NO visible ring on the pill/action buttons.
findings.fix: Adopting `.input` and `.btn-*` (per the consistency finding) gives every control the accent focus ring automatically. For the bare filter-pill `<button>`s, ensure they carry a `.btn-*` or at minimum `focus:ring-2` with the accent ring color so the active filter is keyboard-discoverable.

findings.issue: The status filter pills convey active state only via background color (bg-brand-500 vs bg-brand-100) with no aria-pressed/aria-current. Screen-reader and color-blind users can't tell which filter is active; the count badge inside the pill is the only non-color cue. Sibling materials/filters/tree.html sets aria-current.
findings.fix: Add `aria-pressed="{{ 'true' if status == val else 'false' }}"` (or aria-current) to each filter pill button, matching the materials tree pattern, alongside the accent active-state migration.

findings.issue: Hairlines use border-gray-100 / divide-gray-100 (card action separators, contact divider, disabled pagination buttons) instead of the new line tokens. This is line-debt the page-by-page sweep is meant to drain.
findings.fix: Convert `border-gray-100`/`divide-gray-100` hairlines to `.border-line-subtle` (and the disabled pagination border to `.border-line-base`/`.border-line-subtle`) so they pick up --line-subtle.

findings.issue: The empty state is hand-rolled (bespoke svg + bg-white border border-brand-200 rounded-lg p-6 text-center) rather than using a shared empty-state primitive/pattern used elsewhere, so its padding (p-6/p-8) and look diverge from the rest of the app and from the no-enrichment empty state in detail.html:341.
findings.fix: Standardize on the app's empty-state pattern/`.card` container with consistent padding, and reuse it for both the list empty state and the detail no-enrichment state so they match.

findings.issue: The score progress-bar track is hardcoded `bg-gray-200` with inline `style="width: {{ fit }}%"`. The track gray is fine, but this score-bar markup is duplicated four times across card+detail with no shared component, so any visual tweak (e.g. accent track, rounded ends) must be changed in four places.
findings.fix: Extract the score bar into a Jinja macro in _macros.html (track + fill + width) so card and detail share one definition; the fill color already comes from score_bar_color(). Optional: this is where a foundation score-bar utility would live.

findings.issue: The KPI panel is the page's 'What needs my attention?' answer, but 'Buyer-ready' and 'Call now' (the action-driving numbers) render at the same 2xl weight as the passive 'Suggested'/'Claimed' counts, and none are clickable to filter the grid. The most actionable figures don't stand out and don't act as entry points.
findings.fix: Emphasize the action figures (apply `.figure-accent` to 'Call now' and keep emerald for 'Buyer-ready') and make each KPI card a filter link (hx-get to the grid with the corresponding status/sort) so a buyer can jump straight to the call-now subset — satisfying 'What should I do next?' in one click.

topOpportunities: Single foundation-adoption pass on the cluster: migrate inputs→.input, status/reason/source pills→.badge/.chip, card containers→.card, and all primary/active buttons+filter pills→.btn-primary/accent. This one change clears the high-severity accent debt (24 brand-500/600 usages), fixes the off-brand/missing focus rings, removes the bespoke-primitive smell, and tightens density — the highest leverage move on the page.

Bring the next outreach action onto the card (top decision-maker name + one-click email) so 'the phone is the weapon' holds from the grid — turning a 3-click path into 1 click for the buyer-ready accounts that matter most.

Add a page title/header to the list view and make the KPI tiles (Buyer-ready, Call now) clickable filter entry points with accent emphasis — closing all three legs of the three-second rule (Where am I / What needs attention / What next) for the cheapest effort.
Tighten the vertical rhythm to the post-PR0 locks (stats mb-3 + .card-sm tiles, filters mb-4/space-y-2, grid gap-3) so more ranked prospects sit above the fold on this dense pipeline view.

perPage.summary: My Day is a clean, single-purpose worklist (two sections: "Follow up" accounts + "My tasks") rendered by one self-contained partial with no includes. It already adopts several foundation locks: `.page-readable` shell, the unified `text-xl font-bold` h1, and brand-* correctly used for neutral row chrome/borders. The gaps are: (1) it never uses the accent token anywhere — focus rings, hover states and links all sit on brand-* even though this is exactly the active/focus/CTA surface PR0 says should resolve to accent; (2) it re-implements `.badge` inline four times instead of the primitive; (3) it fails the third part of the Three-Second Rule — there is no next-action/CTA (the "phone is still the weapon" call/email affordance is absent) even though the data to drive it is present; and (4) it drifted from its own sibling (Tasks queue) on metadata contrast (text-gray-400 vs text-gray-600) and dropped the priority badge those rows show.

findings.issue: Three-Second-Rule failure on "what do I do next?". The whole point of My Day is to drive outreach, and the row even shows "Last outbound {{ row.out_days }}d ago" / "Never contacted" (lines 92-96) — but the only action is navigating into the customer page. There is no call/email affordance on the row, contradicting the doctrine that "the phone is still the weapon" and the next outreach action should be ~one click away. A buyer scanning 50 overdue accounts must open each one to act.
findings.fix: Add a right-aligned quick-action on each Follow-up row: a phone/email button (or a small `.btn-secondary` / icon button) using the account's primary contact, plus an inline "Log call" affordance. Reuse the contact/log-call pattern already present in customers/_account_tasks.html rather than inventing new markup, and keep it as one-click as the chevron currently is.

findings.issue: The task-complete checkbox focus ring is `focus:ring-brand-500` (and hover `hover:border-brand-400`). PR0 makes focus rings part of the one-accent system (`.input-focus` / `--accent-ring`). This is the page's only interactive control with a focus ring, so it should resolve to accent, not brand-500. (Note: sibling _account_tasks/_contact_tasks share this exact pattern, so this is app-wide foundation-debt being drained page-by-page — fix here as part of that drain.)
findings.fix: Swap `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-500` for the foundation focus utility `.input-focus` (or `focus:ring-2` with `--tw-ring-color: var(--accent-ring)`), and move the hover to the accent family. Apply the same change to the matching sibling task-checkbox partials so they stay consistent.

findings.issue: Four inline re-implementations of the badge primitive: the section count pills (lines 56-57 rose, 119-120 amber) and the cadence pill (lines 82-85) hand-roll `inline-flex items-center ... px-1.5/px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-bold/medium` instead of using `.badge` / `.badge-warning` / `.badge-mini` from styles.css. This is the bespoke-markup-re-implementing-a-primitive smell called out in the doctrine, and it makes the pills diverge subtly (font-bold here vs font-medium in the .badge family).
findings.fix: Replace the count pills with the `.badge` (or `.badge-mini`) primitive and map the cadence colors through the existing `.badge-warning`/`.badge-success` helpers (or a small {success,warning,danger} map onto `.badge`). Keep the count-pill semantics (rose=overdue urgency, amber=tasks) but route the box model through the shared class so spacing/radius/weight stay locked.

findings.issue: My Day task rows dropped the priority badge that the sibling Tasks queue renders. tasks/_results.html:35-41 shows a High/Low priority pill on each task row; the My Day task row (which uses the identical id scheme my-day-task-{{t.id}} and the same complete endpoint) omits it entirely. A buyer triaging their day can't see which open task is high-priority — institutional-memory/urgency cue lost — and the two surfaces for the same object now look different.
findings.fix: Port the priority badge block from tasks/_results.html:35-41 into the My Day task row (after the title span, line 147), reusing the same markup so the two task surfaces are visually identical. While doing so, convert that pill to the `.badge` primitive per the finding above.

findings.issue: Secondary metadata uses `text-gray-400` (owner/last-outbound line 87, empty section text 110, task linked-entity row 157, empty-task text 179, empty-state subtext 46). The sibling Tasks queue uses `text-gray-600` for the same information (tasks/_results.html:51, list.html:16, _results.html:73). gray-400 on white is below comfortable contrast for this body-adjacent copy and is an in-feature inconsistency (same data, two shades). It also de-emphasizes the exact "Never contacted / 12d ago" institutional-memory signal the page exists to surface.
findings.fix: Bump the metadata/linked-entity/empty-state secondary text from `text-gray-400` to `text-gray-600` to match the Tasks queue and clear the contrast floor. The 'Last outbound Nd ago' / 'Never contacted' string in particular should be at gray-600 so the urgency reads at a glance.

findings.issue: No breadcrumb/'Where am I?' beyond the h1, and the count of overdue accounts only appears as a small pill next to the section header. The single most attention-worthy number for this page (how many accounts are overdue) is not emphasized as a key figure. PR0 provides `.figure-accent` for hero numerics precisely for this.
findings.fix: Consider surfacing the overdue-accounts count as a `.figure-accent` figure in or beside the header (e.g. "3 accounts need a call today"), so the 'What needs my attention?' answer is readable in <3s rather than as an inline pill. Keep it lightweight — one accent numeric, not a stat card grid.

findings.issue: The all-caught-up empty state is bespoke markup (rounded-xl border border-brand-100 bg-brand-50 + inline SVG). There is no shared `.empty-state` class in styles.css, so other pages also hand-roll these — but this one diverges in its border (brand-100) and uses brand-300 for the icon, where most empty states would key the affirmative icon to a semantic/emerald or neutral tone. Minor, and partly a missing-primitive problem rather than this page's fault.
findings.fix: Low priority: align the empty-state container to the same border-line / card treatment used elsewhere (border-line-subtle on bg-white or the established empty-state pattern) and consider an emerald check tone for the affirmative 'all caught up' icon. If/when an `.empty-state` primitive lands, adopt it here. Don't invent a one-off.

topOpportunities: Add a one-click outreach action (call/email + log-call) to each Follow-up row — biggest workflow payoff: it turns My Day from a navigation list into the actual daily call-down tool the doctrine demands ('the phone is still the weapon'), and the driving data (last-outbound days, owner) is already on the row. (hierarchy/high, M)

Restore the priority badge on My Day task rows to match tasks/_results.html, and route it + the count/cadence pills through the shared `.badge` primitive — restores a lost urgency cue and removes 4 bespoke pill re-implementations in one pass. (consistency, S)

Fix contrast/accent debt: bump text-gray-400 metadata to gray-600 (matching the Tasks sibling) and move the task-checkbox focus ring from brand-500 to the accent `.input-focus` token — small, mechanical, and brings the page onto the PR0 accent+readability locks. (a11y + foundation-debt, S)
